opportunities.name: National Lottery Awards for All England
opportunities.description: Grassroots community grants supporting strong relationships, improving places and spaces, and helping people reach their potential. Prioritizes groups with smaller incomes and those addressing cost-of-living challenges. Rolling applications accepted.
opportunities.funder_priorities: The National Lottery Community Fund focuses on supporting people facing poverty, disadvantage or discrimination. They want to create stronger, fairer communities and help communities build connections, reduce loneliness, and create spaces where everyone feels welcome. By 2030, they will share over £4 billion across the UK.
opportunities.what_they_fund: Community activities and projects up to 2 years that bring people together, improve communities, help children and young people thrive, and support healthier living. Funds costs associated with training, equipment, staff time, volunteer expenses, venue hire, and project delivery.
opportunities.what_they_dont_fund: Services which other parts of government have a statutory responsibility to deliver. Activities that don't have a clear community benefit or don't involve the community. Projects that have already taken place.
opportunities.application_process: Rolling applications via online portal. Assessment takes approximately 16 weeks. Simple application form requiring information about your organization, project plans, budget, and how you'll measure success. Groups with smaller incomes are prioritized.

opportunities.name: Asda Foundation Local Community Spaces Fund 2026
opportunities.description: Funding to repair, renovate, or enhance community spaces such as youth clubs, village halls, community cafés and homeless shelters. Supports making spaces functional, welcoming, and sustainable with upgrades like heating, accessibility, structural repairs.
opportunities.funder_priorities: The Asda Foundation prioritizes safe and welcoming community spaces that bring people together, connect communities, and address loneliness and isolation. They want to support spaces that serve diverse groups throughout the year and demonstrate clear community benefit.
opportunities.what_they_fund: Capital projects for repairing, maintaining, and improving community buildings and outdoor spaces. Heating systems, accessibility improvements, structural repairs, refurbishment of key facilities. Projects must be completed by December 2027.
opportunities.what_they_dont_fund: General running costs, projects already completed, statutory services, spaces that charge participants without consideration of ability to pay.
opportunities.application_process: Two-stage process: complete eligibility check online, then register on grant management system and submit detailed application with financial records, governance documents, safeguarding policies, and project plans. Shortlisted applications undergo two-stage review. Decisions by 31 August 2026. May close early if oversubscribed.

opportunities.name: John Horniman's Children's Trust
opportunities.description: Four-year grants supporting organizations assisting the relief of sickness and advancement of education for children who are sick, convalescent or have learning disabilities. Provides sustained funding for established work.
opportunities.funder_priorities: The Trust focuses on supporting sick and disadvantaged children. They want to see sustained impact through multi-year funding that allows charities to plan ahead and develop their services. Priority for children who are sick, convalescent, or have learning disabilities.
opportunities.what_they_fund: Multi-year grants (four years) for registered UK charities and CIOs working with sick children or children with learning disabilities. Support for both project costs and some organizational development.
opportunities.what_they_dont_fund: CICs, individuals, non-UK organizations, projects outside their core focus on sick children and learning disabilities.
opportunities.application_process: Expression of Interest deadline 5pm Thursday 30th April 2026. Two-stage process with initial EOI followed by full application if shortlisted. Must be registered charity or CIO.

opportunities.name: The Fore Funding Rounds
opportunities.description: Unrestricted grants to help small charities and social enterprises grow, strengthen, become more efficient or resilient. Designed for organizational development rather than project funding. Includes access to free skilled professional support.
opportunities.deadline: 8 July 2026 (registration opens), 15 July 2026 (registration closes) for Autumn 2026 round
opportunities.funder_priorities: The Fore focuses on helping small charities and social enterprises take the next step in their growth or sustainability. They want to strengthen organizations internally through unrestricted funding combined with skilled professional support. They value innovation, impact, and organizational sustainability.
opportunities.what_they_fund: Unrestricted grants that can be used for any purpose that strengthens your organization - staffing, systems, strategy development, reserves, equipment. Must demonstrate the grant will help you take next step forwards in growth or sustainability. Registered charities, CIOs, CICs, and Community Benefit Societies eligible.
opportunities.what_they_dont_fund: Project-specific funding, organizations that don't meet size criteria, capital building projects, individuals.
opportunities.application_process: Registration window opens 12pm Wednesday 8 July to 12pm Wednesday 15 July 2026. Must register to apply during this window. Then complete full application. Assessment includes interviews and organizational review. Includes free access to highly skilled professional support alongside funding.

opportunities.name: King Charles III Charitable Fund - Environment and Countryside
opportunities.description: Multi-year grants supporting environmental and countryside projects. Designed for small to medium charities working on environmental sustainability, heritage landscapes, and countryside access.
opportunities.amount: Up to £3,000 per year (up to 3 years)
opportunities.funder_priorities: The Fund focuses on environment and countryside themes, supporting sustainable practices, heritage conservation, and community access to countryside. They want to see projects that engage communities with their local environment and heritage.
opportunities.what_they_fund: Projects under environment and countryside funding themes. Multi-year grants for registered charities and CICs with annual income between £25,000 and £500,000. Supports both project and some core costs.
opportunities.what_they_dont_fund: Organizations outside income bracket (under £25,000 or over £500,000), individuals, non-environmental projects.
opportunities.application_process: Deadline Thursday 7 May 2026. Online application form requiring project details, budget, organizational information, and impact measurement plans. Decision communicated within standard timeframe.

opportunities.name: Grocers' Charity Grant 2026
opportunities.description: One-off grants for UK registered charities across nine sectors including relief of hardship (training courses, homelessness support), children and young people, elderly support, disability, health, military, arts, heritage, and environment/conservation.
opportunities.funder_priorities: The Grocers' Charity supports a wide range of charitable causes particularly focusing on relief of hardship including training courses and parenting support. They prioritize work in areas of high deprivation, projects that empower disadvantaged people, and activities that build strengths and potential in children and young people.
opportunities.what_they_fund: Direct project costs for training courses, better facilities, parenting support, homelessness prevention, domestic abuse support, youth development, befriending services for elderly, disability inclusion. UK registered charities with annual turnover under £500,000 (or £15 million for health/medical).
opportunities.what_they_dont_fund: Places of worship, schools, colleges, universities, individuals, non-UK organizations, CICs, charities with overseas beneficiaries, organizations over income limits.
opportunities.application_process: Two-stage system. Stage 1: Initial Enquiry - complete online eligibility checklist and enquiry form. Stage 2: Full application if invited. Applications close 1 September 2026. Must gather documents early for both stages.

opportunities.name: Skinners' Charity Foundation - Young People's Vocational Development
opportunities.description: Grants for organizations helping NEET young people get ready for and move into employment. Focus on vocational training, employability skills, and work readiness. Priority areas around Tonbridge, Tunbridge Wells, Romney Marsh, Sevenoaks but will fund in other Kent areas.
opportunities.amount: Up to £10,000 per year (3 years available)
opportunities.funder_priorities: The Skinners' Charity Foundation prioritizes helping NEET young people prepare for and move into employment. They focus on practical vocational development, work-readiness programs, and support that creates pathways to sustainable employment. Strong Kent focus with priority areas including Thanet region.
opportunities.what_they_fund: Organizations which help NEET young people get ready for and move into employment through vocational training and support. Grants of up to £10,000 per year available with awards for three years open to consideration. Also Relief of Poverty programme with up to £5,000 per year for three years.
opportunities.what_they_dont_fund: Individuals, organizations without clear employment outcomes, projects outside their geographical areas of interest.
opportunities.application_process: Rolling applications accepted. Contact the foundation to discuss eligibility and approach. Priority areas around Tonbridge, Tunbridge Wells, Romney Marsh and Sevenoaks but will fund in other areas of Kent including Thanet.

opportunities.name: The Gibbons Family Trust
opportunities.description: Awards grants to children and young people up to age 25 living in the Isle of Thanet in Kent. Local grant-making trust specifically for Thanet area supporting youth development and opportunities.
opportunities.funder_priorities: The Gibbons Family Trust focuses exclusively on supporting children and young people up to age 25 in the Isle of Thanet. They want to improve opportunities and life chances for young Thanet residents through accessible local grant-making.
opportunities.what_they_fund: Projects and support for children and young people up to age 25 living in the Isle of Thanet, Kent. Wide range of youth development activities, education support, and opportunity creation.
opportunities.what_they_dont_fund: Applications from outside Isle of Thanet area, support for individuals over 25, general organizational running costs unrelated to youth work.
opportunities.application_process: Apply through Kent Community Foundation. Rolling applications. Contact Kent Community Foundation grants team for guidance and eligibility check before applying.

opportunities.name: Kent Community Foundation - Various Funds
opportunities.description: Kent's local community foundation distributing over £60 million to grassroots charities and community groups. Multiple funds available for Kent and Medway organizations across various themes. Provides grants AND loans (£10,000-£100,000 with up to 30% grant).
opportunities.amount: Varies by fund - small grants to £100,000
opportunities.funder_priorities: Kent Community Foundation partners with local donors to fund community projects creating lasting change across Kent and Medway. Their work is guided by local insight and deep understanding of community needs. They focus on supporting people and places with greatest need across Kent and Medway.
opportunities.what_they_fund: Wide range of charitable activities in Kent and Medway. Must be constituted group with charitable purposes including registered charities, CICs, companies limited by guarantee with charitable aims. Also offers unsecured loans £10,000-£100,000 with up to 30% as grant for organizations looking to grow, innovate or sustain impact.
opportunities.what_they_dont_fund: Statutory organizations unless clearly outside state obligations, CICs with PSCs holding unequal powers, schools (though Friends of groups welcome).
opportunities.application_process: Use online Eligibility Checker first. Multiple funds available with different deadlines. Contact Kent Community Foundation grants team for guidance. Friendly support provided throughout application process. Requires governing document, safeguarding policies if working with vulnerable groups, and completion of previous grant monitoring if applicable.

opportunities.name: Colyer-Fergusson Charitable Trust - Investing in Communities
opportunities.description: Grants to build the capacity of community organizations and local charities working with vulnerable people in Kent. Focus on organizational development to help deliver sustainable, face-to-face services.
opportunities.deadline: Check with trust for current round
opportunities.funder_priorities: The Colyer-Fergusson Charitable Trust focuses on building the capacity of community organizations and local charities in Kent. They want to see sustainable, face-to-face services for vulnerable people. Strong emphasis on organizational strengthening and long-term service delivery.
opportunities.what_they_fund: Capacity building for community organizations and local charities working with vulnerable people in Kent. Focus on helping organizations deliver sustainable, face-to-face services. Up to £15,000 grants available for organizational development.
opportunities.what_they_dont_fund: Organizations outside Kent, capital projects without organizational development element, services not focused on vulnerable people.
opportunities.application_process: Contact the trust for current application rounds and deadlines. Likely to require organizational assessment, clear capacity-building plan, and demonstration of work with vulnerable people in Kent.

opportunities.name: Better Community Business Network Grant Initiative
opportunities.description: Small grants supporting charities and community-led projects across UK. One-off awards to improve quality of life and opportunities for individuals and communities, particularly those experiencing social or economic disadvantage.
opportunities.funder_priorities: The Better Community Business Network focuses on improving quality of life and opportunities for individuals and communities, particularly those experiencing social or economic disadvantage. They support accessible, community-led initiatives that create practical improvements.
opportunities.what_they_fund: One-off awards (£36,000 total annual allocation distributed through 12 grants yearly). Community groups, voluntary organisations, charities, local branches of national charities, and organizations with charitable aims. Must be UK-based and registered with recognized governing body for at least 18 months with submitted annual accounts. Education, health, ex-offenders, homelessness, poverty, arts, environment, elderly, disability, social cohesion.
opportunities.what_they_dont_fund: Organizations not registered for 18 months, groups without submitted accounts, projects outside UK.
opportunities.application_process: Three deadlines in 2026: 17 April, 7 September, 9 November. Online application form. Must be registered with Charity Commission or Companies House for at least 18 months. Simple application process for small grants.

opportunities.name: Help the Homeless Capital Projects Fund
opportunities.description: Small grant-making organization supporting UK charities helping people experiencing homelessness reintegrate into community and rebuild lives. Grants for capital projects such as refurbishment costs and furniture.
opportunities.funder_priorities: Help the Homeless supports people experiencing homelessness in reintegrating into the community and rebuilding their lives. Focus on practical, capital support that creates safe spaces and functional facilities for homeless people.
opportunities.what_they_fund: Capital projects including refurbishment costs and furniture for charities working wholly or mostly with homeless people. Small and medium-sized registered charities with annual turnover below £500,000. Must be registered with Charity Commission in England and Wales, Northern Ireland, or Office of the Scottish Charity Regulator.
opportunities.what_they_dont_fund: Revenue costs, general running costs, organizations over £500,000 turnover, non-registered groups, services not focused on homelessness.
opportunities.application_process: Quarterly deadlines - next deadline 20 June 2026. Online application form available at Help the Homeless website. Straightforward application for small capital grants.

opportunities.name: Leathersellers' Small Grants Programme 2025-26
opportunities.description: Rolling funding programme for one-off grants to small charities with annual income under £200k holding less than six months reserves. For excellent organizations making difference in vulnerable communities. Extremely competitive - fills quickly.
opportunities.deadline: Next window opens 10am Tuesday 5 May 2026 (closes when 40 applications received)
opportunities.funder_priorities: Leathersellers want to fund excellent small organizations making measurable difference in vulnerable people's lives. Strong emphasis on evidenced outcomes, financial need, and direct community impact. They prioritize charities holding 6 months or less reserves due to high demand from organizations with financial need.
opportunities.what_they_fund: Unrestricted grants for small charities and CIOs (not CICs) with income under £200k and no more than 6 months reserves. Must deliver activities for vulnerable community members with evidenced impact. Funding for organization as whole, not individual projects. Eight application windows yearly, each closes at 40 applications.
opportunities.what_they_dont_fund: CICs, medical research, life-threatening medical conditions, services charging participants regardless of ability to pay, charities over £200k income or holding more than 6 months reserves.
opportunities.application_process: Eight windows yearly, each closes when 40 applications received. December 2025 window filled in under 20 minutes. Next opens 10am Tuesday 5 May 2026. MUST prepare application offline using Word template. Committee meeting 16 June 2026. Extremely competitive - apply immediately when window opens.

opportunities.name: AB Charitable Trust Open Programme
opportunities.description: Unrestricted grants for registered charities promoting human dignity and defending human rights of marginalised and excluded people. Focus on human rights framework, access to justice, criminal legal system and penal reform, and migrants and refugees.
opportunities.funder_priorities: AB Charitable Trust promotes human dignity and defends human rights of marginalised and excluded people. Strong focus on systemic issues around human rights, justice system, and support for migrants. Values unrestricted funding that allows organizations flexibility.
opportunities.what_they_fund: Unrestricted grants for registered charities with annual income between £150,000 and £1.5 million. Support for work under: Human Rights Framework, Access to Justice, Criminal Legal System and Penal Reform, and Migrants and Refugees. Multi-year funding available.
opportunities.what_they_dont_fund: Organizations outside income bracket, non-registered charities, work outside their four thematic areas, CICs.
opportunities.application_process: Deadline Friday 24 April 2026. Online application requiring organizational information, description of work, how it promotes human dignity and defends human rights, and budget details. Multi-year grants available so application should address long-term sustainability.
